title: Glowing Peppery Japanese Freekeh Tsukune
description: A peppery and fluffy Japanese dish featuring freekeh, flambe, and ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients, served in a copper mug.
instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the toasted freekeh and cook for 2 minutes, until lightly toasted.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce and mirin.
Pour the soy sauce mixture over the freekeh and stir to combine.
Add the black pepper, chopped scallions, and sesame seeds, and stir to combine.
Using a small ice cream scoop or spoon, shape the freekeh mixture into small tsukune balls.
Place the tsukune balls in a copper mug and flambe with a small amount of sesame oil.
Sprinkle the shichimi togarashi over the top of the tsukune balls and serve immediately.
